Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Inline Note Creation on Freedom Wall Canvas

## Context
Currently, clicking the canvas with the "note" tool opens a modal overlay to design and type a note. The user wants notes to appear directly on the canvas instead, where they can be dragged to reposition while being designed via an inline panel. Character limit reduced from 800 to 200.

## Files to Modify
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css`

## Changes

### 1. Replace `noteModal` state with `draftNote` state
- Rename `noteModal` → `draftNote` (same shape: x, y, text, fontFamily, fontStyle, fontColor, bgColor, fontSize)
- Default `text` to `""` instead of `"New note"`

### 2. Render draft note directly on the Konva canvas
- Add a `NoteNode` in the items Layer (after all sorted items) when `draftNote` is set
- Pass `isDraft={true}` prop to `NoteNode` for visual distinction (dashed purple border)
- Make it draggable — `onDragEnd` updates `draftNote.x` and `draftNote.y`

### 3. Add `isDraft` prop to `NoteNode` component
- When `isDraft` is true: dashed stroke border on the main note body shape

### 4. Replace modal JSX with inline design panel
- Remove the full-screen modal overlay (`fw-note-modal-overlay`)
- Add an inline `fw-draft-note-panel` between toolbar and canvas with:
  - Character count display (X/200)
  - Textarea (maxLength=200, autoFocus)
  - Font, style, font color, note color, font size controls
  - "Place Note" (disabled when empty) and "Cancel" buttons
- All changes update `draftNote` state → canvas note updates live

### 5. Update handlers
- `handlePlaceItem`: set `draftNote` instead of `noteModal`, guard against double-drafting
- New `handleDraftNoteSave`: creates wall item from `draftNote`, clears it
- `handleStagePointerDown`: skip placing another note if `draftNote` already active

### 6. Auto-cancel draft on tool switch
- `useEffect` clears `draftNote` when `activeTool` changes away from `"note"`

### 7. Update character limits
- New draft textarea: `maxLength={200}`
- Existing note editor textarea: `maxLength={800}` → `maxLength={200}`

### 8. Add CSS for inline draft panel
- `.fw-draft-note-panel` — card-style, inline (not overlay), matches existing toolbar styling
- Responsive: controls stack vertically at 480px

### 9. Remove dead modal code
- Remove all `noteModal`/`setNoteModal` references
- Remove `handleNoteModalSave`
- Remove modal overlay JSX
- Remove modal CSS classes (`.fw-note-modal-*`)

## Verification
- Click canvas with note tool → note appears on canvas with dashed border, inline panel shows
- Type text → canvas note updates live
- Change font/color/style → canvas note reflects changes instantly
- Drag note on canvas → repositions correctly
- "Place Note" → saves to backend, draft clears, note persists
- "Cancel" → draft clears, no note saved
- Switch to another tool while drafting → draft auto-cancels
- Click canvas again while draft active → ignored (no duplicate drafts)
- Character count shows and enforces 200 char limit

planContent:
# Plan: Inline Note Creation on Freedom Wall Canvas

## Context
Currently, clicking the canvas with the "note" tool opens a modal overlay to design and type a note. The user wants notes to appear directly on the canvas instead, where they can be dragged to reposition while being designed via an inline panel. Character limit reduced from 800 to 200.

## Files to Modify
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css`

## Changes

### 1. Replace `noteModal` state with `draftNote` state
- Rename `noteModal` → `draftNote` (same shape: x, y, text, fontFamily, fontStyle, fontColor, bgColor, fontSize)
- Default `text` to `""` instead of `"New note"`

### 2. Render draft note directly on the Konva canvas
- Add a `NoteNode` in the items Layer (after all sorted items) when `draftNote` is set
- Pass `isDraft={true}` prop to `NoteNode` for visual distinction (dashed purple border)
- Make it draggable — `onDragEnd` updates `draftNote.x` and `draftNote.y`

### 3. Add `isDraft` prop to `NoteNode` component
- When `isDraft` is true: dashed stroke border on the main note body shape

### 4. Replace modal JSX with inline design panel
- Remove the full-screen modal overlay (`fw-note-modal-overlay`)
- Add an inline `fw-draft-note-panel` between toolbar and canvas with:
  - Character count display (X/200)
  - Textarea (maxLength=200, autoFocus)
  - Font, style, font color, note color, font size controls
  - "Place Note" (disabled when empty) and "Cancel" buttons
- All changes update `draftNote` state → canvas note updates live

### 5. Update handlers
- `handlePlaceItem`: set `draftNote` instead of `noteModal`, guard against double-drafting
- New `handleDraftNoteSave`: creates wall item from `draftNote`, clears it
- `handleStagePointerDown`: skip placing another note if `draftNote` already active

### 6. Auto-cancel draft on tool switch
- `useEffect` clears `draftNote` when `activeTool` changes away from `"note"`

### 7. Update character limits
- New draft textarea: `maxLength={200}`
- Existing note editor textarea: `maxLength={800}` → `maxLength={200}`

### 8. Add CSS for inline draft panel
- `.fw-draft-note-panel` — card-style, inline (not overlay), matches existing toolbar styling
- Responsive: controls stack vertically at 480px

### 9. Remove dead modal code
- Remove all `noteModal`/`setNoteModal` references
- Remove `handleNoteModalSave`
- Remove modal overlay JSX
- Remove modal CSS classes (`.fw-note-modal-*`)

## Verification
- Click canvas with note tool → note appears on canvas with dashed border, inline panel shows
- Type text → canvas note updates live
- Change font/color/style → canvas note reflects changes instantly
- Drag note on canvas → repositions correctly
- "Place Note" → saves to backend, draft clears, note persists
- "Cancel" → draft clears, no note saved
- Switch to another tool while drafting → draft auto-cancels
- Click canvas again while draft active → ignored (no duplicate drafts)
- Character count shows and enforces 200 char limit

All the changes are complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**FreedomWallPage.jsx:**
1. **`noteModal` → `draftNote`** state rename, default text changed from `"New note"` to `""`
2. **`NoteNode` gets `isDraft` prop** — when true, renders with a dashed purple (`#a21caf`) border via `dash={[6, 4]}`
3. **Draft note rendered on canvas** — appears after sorted items, draggable with `onDragEnd` updating `draftNote.x`/`y`
4. **Modal replaced with inline panel** (`fw-draft-note-panel`) — shows between toolbar and canvas with character count (X/200), textarea, font/style/color/size controls, and Place Note (disabled when empty) + Cancel buttons
5. **Guard against double-drafting** — `handlePlaceItem` returns early if `draftNote` already set; `handleStagePointerDown` skips note placement when draft active
6. **Auto-cancel on tool switch** — `useEffect` clears `draftNote` when `activeTool` changes away from `"note"`
7. **Character limit reduced** — both draft textarea and note editor textarea now `maxLength={200}`

**freedomWall.css:**
- Removed all `.fw-note-modal-*` classes (overlay, modal, preview, textarea, controls, rows, actions)
- Added `.fw-draft-note-panel` and child classes — inline card matching existing toolbar styling with purple-tinted border
- Responsive: controls stack vertically at 480px

message.content:
can you do this optimization? . Interaction Feedback (The "Tactile" Feel)
If the wall uses the Supabase + Konva stack we planned, the biggest risk is it feeling "static" or laggy.

Improvement: Implement Haptic/Visual feedback when a sticker is placed. A small "puff" of digital smoke or a slight screen shake makes the act of "vandalizing" the wall feel much more satisfying.

Improvement: Add Cursors of Other Users. Seeing 5–10 other little "pencil" icons moving around the wall in real-time creates a sense of "digital presence" that is missing from a standard scrolling feed.

# Plan: Inline Note Creation on Freedom Wall Canvas

## Context
Currently, clicking the canvas with the "note" tool opens a modal overlay to design and type a note. The user wants notes to appear directly on the canvas instead, where they can be dragged to reposition while being designed via an inline panel. Character limit reduced from 800 to 200.

## Files to Modify
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css`

## Changes

### 1. Replace `noteModal` state with `draftNote` state
- Rename `noteModal` → `draftNote` (same shape: x, y, text, fontFamily, fontStyle, fontColor, bgColor, fontSize)
- Default `text` to `""` instead of `"New note"`

### 2. Render draft note directly on the Konva canvas
- Add a `NoteNode` in the items Layer (after all sorted items) when `draftNote` is set
- Pass `isDraft={true}` prop to `NoteNode` for visual distinction (dashed purple border)
- Make it draggable — `onDragEnd` updates `draftNote.x` and `draftNote.y`

### 3. Add `isDraft` prop to `NoteNode` component
- When `isDraft` is true: dashed stroke border on the main note body shape

### 4. Replace modal JSX with inline design panel
- Remove the full-screen modal overlay (`fw-note-modal-overlay`)
- Add an inline `fw-draft-note-panel` between toolbar and canvas with:
  - Character count display (X/200)
  - Textarea (maxLength=200, autoFocus)
  - Font, style, font color, note color, font size controls
  - "Place Note" (disabled when empty) and "Cancel" buttons
- All changes update `draftNote` state → canvas note updates live

### 5. Update handlers
- `handlePlaceItem`: set `draftNote` instead of `noteModal`, guard against double-drafting
- New `handleDraftNoteSave`: creates wall item from `draftNote`, clears it
- `handleStagePointerDown`: skip placing another note if `draftNote` already active

### 6. Auto-cancel draft on tool switch
- `useEffect` clears `draftNote` when `activeTool` changes away from `"note"`

### 7. Update character limits
- New draft textarea: `maxLength={200}`
- Existing note editor textarea: `maxLength={800}` → `maxLength={200}`

### 8. Add CSS for inline draft panel
- `.fw-draft-note-panel` — card-style, inline (not overlay), matches existing toolbar styling
- Responsive: controls stack vertically at 480px

### 9. Remove dead modal code
- Remove all `noteModal`/`setNoteModal` references
- Remove `handleNoteModalSave`
- Remove modal overlay JSX
- Remove modal CSS classes (`.fw-note-modal-*`)

## Verification
- Click canvas with note tool → note appears on canvas with dashed border, inline panel shows
- Type text → canvas note updates live
- Change font/color/style → canvas note reflects changes instantly
- Drag note on canvas → repositions correctly
- "Place Note" → saves to backend, draft clears, note persists
- "Cancel" → draft clears, no note saved
- Switch to another tool while drafting → draft auto-cancels
- Click canvas again while draft active → ignored (no duplicate drafts)
- Character count shows and enforces 200 char limit

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive exploration report.

## Freedom Wall Feature Exploration Report

Based on a thorough analysis of the codebase, here's what I found:

### 1. **Konva Stage/Layer Structure** (FreedomWallPage.jsx)

The Freedom Wall uses a sophisticated Konva rendering structure with **two main layers**:

**Background/Grid Layer (non-listening)**
- Static grid rendering with vertical and horizontal lines at 36px intervals (GRID_SIZE)
- Backdrop rectangle with light blue background: `rgba(205, 250, 255, 0.22)`
- Uses `listening={false}` for performance (no interaction)

**Interactive Content Layer**
- Renders four types of items dynamically:
  1. **Doodles**: `<Line>` with normalized points, customizable color/size
  2. **Stickers**: `<StickerNode>` (emoji-based via canvas rendering), draggable
  3. **Stamps**: `<StampNode>` (small emoji icons), draggable  
  4. **Notes**: `<NoteNode>` (complex Group with Shape-based rendering - skewed rectangles, push pins, fold effects)

- **Note Node Structure** (lines 218-331): Uses Konva `Shape` for custom SVG-like drawing:
  - Shadow layer (dark overlay beneath)
  - Main note body with customizable background color
  - Page curl triangle (darkened corner effect)
  - Curl fold shadow (depth effect)
  - Text content with font controls
  - Two red push pins with glossy highlights

**Draft Items Rendering**:
- Temporary items shown with dashed borders (`dash={[6, 4]}`)
- Draft notes/stickers/stamps render with `isDraft={true}` flag
- Live doodle line shown during drawing

### 2. **createItemMutation Flow** (Optimistic Updates)

Located in lines 507-554:

```javascript
onMutate: Creates optimistic item immediately
  - Generates optimistic ID: `optimistic-${Date.now()}-${random}`
  - Constructs full item object with current user data
  - Updates cache via setItemsCache()
  
onSuccess: Replaces optimistic with real item
  - Removes optimistic ID from cache
  - Inserts actual server response via upsertById()
  
onError: Rolls back on failure
  - Removes optimistic item from cache
  - Sets error toast with 4-second timeout
```

**Helper functions**:
- `upsertById()`: Merges payloads and preserves user info (lines 62-80)
- `removeById()`: Filters items by ID (line 82)
- `setItemsCache()`: React Query cache updater (lines 490-505)

### 3. **Supabase Realtime Subscription** (lines 622-675)

Channel setup pattern:
```javascript
const channel = supabase
  .channel(`freedom-wall-${weekId}`)
  .on("postgres_changes", 
    {event: "*", schema: "public", table: "freedom_wall_items", 
     filter: `week_id=eq.${weekId}`},
    (payload) => { /* upsert/remove logic */ }
  )
  .subscribe();
```

**Event handling**:
- **DELETE**: Removes item via `removeById()`
- **INSERT/UPDATE**: Normalizes user data, then `upsertById()`
- **Soft deletes**: Checks for `deleted_at` field, removes if present

**Cleanup**: `supabase.removeChannel(channel)` on unmount

---

### 4. **Supabase Presence/Broadcast Usage in Codebase**

Found **active usage in CanvasViewer.jsx** (NOT in Freedom Wall, but in the Canvas feature):

**Broadcast Implementation** (lines 546-590):
```javascript
channel.send({
  type: "broadcast",
  event: eventName,
  payload: payload
})
```

**Live Doodle Broadcasting**:
- Event: `doodle-progress` - sends ongoing drawing points every 40ms
- Event: `doodle-end` - broadcasts completion with final points
- Uses queuing + throttling to manage bandwidth
- Listeners track `liveDoodlesByClient` state to show other users' live doodles

**Presence is NOT used** - Only `postgres_changes` + `broadcast` channels.

---

### 5. **Framer Motion Availability**

**Status: INSTALLED & AVAILABLE**

From package.json (line 23):
```json
"framer-motion": "^12.23.23"
```

**Current usage in project**:
- Imported in 33 files across components (HomePage, Profile, Notifications, etc.)
- Used for: page transitions, modal animations, sidebar toggles, button hover effects
- **NOT currently used in FreedomWallPage.jsx** - only basic CSS transitions

---

### 6. **Supabase Client Configuration**

**File**: `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\utils\supabaseClient.js`

```javascript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';

const supabase = createClient(
    import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL,
    import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_API_KEY
)
export default supabase;
```

**Configuration**:
- Environment variables: `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and `VITE_SUPABASE_API_KEY`
- Standard Supabase JS client v2.75.1
- No custom options/security policies defined at client level
- Supports both `postgres_changes` (realtime DB) and `broadcast` (custom events)

---

### 7. **CSS Animation/Transition Classes** (freedomWall.css)

**No animation classes found** - only:
- Layout & styling (flexbox, colors, borders)
- Button states (`.is-active`)
- Responsive design
- Color-mix utilities for hover effects

**Opportunity**: Freedom Wall UI is currently static - animations could enhance:
- Item placement (pop-in/fade effects)
- Selection feedback
- Draft item appearance
- Realtime updates visibility

---

## Summary Table

| Feature | Status | Location | Details |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| Konva Stage/Layer | Implemented | Lines 1268-1465 | 2 layers, 4 item types, draft rendering |
| Optimistic Updates | Implemented | Lines 507-554 | Full flow with error rollback |
| Realtime Subscription | Implemented | Lines 622-675 | Postgres_changes only, no Presence |
| Framer Motion | Available | package.json line 23 | v12.23.23, not used in Freedom Wall |
| Supabase Broadcast | Available | CanvasViewer.jsx | Used for live doodle sync, not Freedom Wall |
| CSS Animations | Minimal | freedomWall.css | No animations, opportunity for enhancement |
| Supabase Config | Simple | supabaseClient.js | Basic client setup, env vars only |

Now I have a solid understanding of both features. Let me design the plan.

For the two improvements:

1. **Placement "puff" animation** - When an item is placed on the canvas, show a visual feedback effect. Since Konva doesn't easily support CSS animations, I have two approaches:
   - Use a Konva-based animation (Circle that expands and fades out)
   - Use Framer Motion for HTML overlay effects
   
   The Konva approach is better since the effect needs to appear at the exact canvas coordinates. I can create a temporary "puff" effect using Konva shapes that animate (scale up + fade out) using Konva's built-in tweening or requestAnimationFrame.
   
2. **Live cursors** - I'll use Supabase Broadcast on the existing `freedom-wall-${weekId}` channel to transmit cursor positions and display other users' cursors as small icons on the canvas. The tricky part is that the current channel only listens for `postgres_changes`, so I need to add a `.on("broadcast", ...)` listener before calling `.subscribe()`. For the puff animation specifically, I can trigger it during the optimistic update when `createItemMutation.onMutate` fires, positioning it at the item's coordinates.

The puff itself would be a Konva Circle or Ring that scales up and fades out over about 400ms using either `requestAnimationFrame` or Konva's built-in `Tween` system, with support for multiple simultaneous puffs stored in an array. For live cursors, I'll throttle `cursor-move` broadcasts at 60-80ms intervals, sending the client ID, user info, coordinates, tool, and timestamp. Each cursor renders as a small colored dot with a label showing the user's name, and I'll auto-cleanup stale cursors after 3 seconds of inactivity. Broadcasting should only happen when the mouse is actually over the canvas.

I'm still deciding whether the puff effect should be a simple particle system or just a single animated shape.

Actually, an expanding ring is simpler and more performant, so I'll go with that plus a few small particles for visual appeal without overcomplicating things. For the cursor display, I could show different icons per tool, but keeping it simple with just a colored dot and username label makes more sense, maybe with a small tool indicator on the side.

input.content:
# Plan: Freedom Wall Interaction Feedback & Live Cursors

## Context
The Freedom Wall canvas currently feels static — items appear instantly without visual feedback, and there's no sense of other users being present. Two improvements will make it feel alive: a "puff" particle effect when items are placed, and real-time cursor broadcasting so users can see each other moving around the wall.

## Files to Modify
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css`

---

## Feature 1: Placement Puff Animation

Visual particle burst when any item (sticker, stamp, note, doodle) is placed on the canvas.

### Implementation

**1. Add `placementPuffs` state array**
- Shape: `[{id, x, y, startedAt}]` — pixel coordinates, not normalized
- Each puff auto-removes after animation completes (~500ms)

**2. Create `PuffEffect` Konva component**
- Renders inside the items `<Layer>` (after all items, on top)
- Uses `useRef` + `requestAnimationFrame` loop to animate:
  - 5–8 small `<Circle>` particles that fly outward from center, shrinking + fading
  - 1 central expanding `<Ring>` that fades out
- Each particle gets a random angle, travels outward ~30-50px over 500ms
- Colors: soft purple/pink tones matching the accent (`#a21caf`, `#e879f9`)
- On animation complete, calls `onComplete(id)` to remove from state

**3. Trigger puffs on item creation**
- In `createItemMutation.onMutate`: calculate pixel position from the normalized `x, y` payload, push a new puff into state
- Convert normalized coords → pixel: `x * stageWidth`, `y * stageHeight`
- For doodles: use the midpoint of the drawn stroke

**4. Render puffs on canvas**
- After all items in the Layer, map `placementPuffs` → `<PuffEffect>` components
- Each `PuffEffect` self-animates and self-destructs via the `onComplete` callback

---

## Feature 2: Live Cursors via Supabase Broadcast

Show other users' cursor positions on the canvas in real-time.

### Implementation

**1. Generate a stable client ID**
- `useRef` initialized once: `fw-client-${Date.now()}-${random}`
- Used to filter out own cursor from rendering

**2. Extend the existing Supabase channel**
- The `freedom-wall-${weekId}` channel already exists for `postgres_changes`
- Chain `.on("broadcast", {event: "cursor-move"}, handler)` before `.subscribe()`
- No new channel needed

**3. Broadcast cursor position (throttled)**
- On `onMouseMove` / `onTouchMove` over the Stage (separate from doodle handling):
  - Compute normalized position `{x: px/stageWidth, y: py/stageHeight}`
  - Throttle to every **80ms** using a ref + setTimeout pattern (same as CanvasViewer's `queueDoodleBroadcast`)
  - Payload: `{clientId, userId, userName, x, y, tool: activeTool, timestamp}`
- On mouse leave Stage: broadcast a `cursor-leave` event to remove cursor
- Only broadcast when `canWrite` is true (logged-in users)

**4. Track remote cursors in state**
- `const [remoteCursors, setRemoteCursors] = useState({})` — keyed by clientId
- Shape: `{clientId, userId, userName, x, y, tool, updatedAt}`
- On `cursor-move` broadcast received: upsert into state (skip own clientId)
- On `cursor-leave` received: remove from state

**5. Auto-cleanup stale cursors**
- `setInterval` every 1.5s, remove cursors with `updatedAt` older than 3s
- Handles disconnects/tab-closes where `cursor-leave` never fires

**6. Render remote cursors on canvas**
- New `<Layer>` on top of the items layer (non-interactive: `listening={false}`)
- For each remote cursor:
  - Small colored `<Circle>` (radius 5, fill based on `userId` hash for consistent color)
  - `<Text>` label showing `userName` (truncated to 10 chars), offset slightly right+below the dot
  - Position: `x * stageWidth`, `y * stageHeight`
- Limit rendering to max 15 cursors (sorted by most recent `updatedAt`)

**7. Broadcast cleanup on unmount**
- Send `cursor-leave` event before channel teardown
- Clear interval timer

**8. CSS for cursor layer (HTML overlay for name labels — optional)**
- No CSS needed; all rendering is within Konva canvas layers

---

## Detailed Change Map

### State additions
```
const [placementPuffs, setPlacementPuffs] = useState([]);
const [remoteCursors, setRemoteCursors] = useState({});
const clientIdRef = useRef(`fw-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2,8)}`);
const cursorBroadcastTimerRef = useRef(null);
const lastCursorBroadcastRef = useRef(0);
const realtimeChannelRef = useRef(null);
```

### Channel modification (existing useEffect at line ~622)
- Add `.on("broadcast", {event: "cursor-move"}, handler)` and `.on("broadcast", {event: "cursor-leave"}, handler)` to the channel chain
- Store channel in `realtimeChannelRef` for broadcast sending
- On cleanup: send `cursor-leave`, then `removeChannel`

### New components (inside same file)
- `PuffEffect` — self-animating Konva Group with particles
- `RemoteCursor` — Konva Group with Circle + Text for one cursor

### New Konva Layer (after items Layer)
```jsx
<Layer listening={false}>
    {remoteCursorItems.map(cursor => <RemoteCursor key={cursor.clientId} ... />)}
</Layer>
```

### Puff trigger location
- Inside `createItemMutation.onMutate` callback, after creating the optimistic item:
```js
const pxX = (payload.payload.x || 0.5) * stageWidth;
const pxY = (payload.payload.y || 0.5) * stageHeight;
setPlacementPuffs(prev => [...prev, {id: optimisticId, x: pxX, y: pxY, startedAt: Date.now()}]);
```

### Cursor broadcast location
- New `handleCursorMove` called from Stage `onMouseMove` (runs alongside existing `handleStagePointerMove` for doodles — separate concern, both fire)
- Uses throttle pattern: skip if <80ms since last broadcast

---

## Verification
- Place a sticker/stamp/note → particle puff bursts at placement point, fades in ~500ms
- Draw a doodle → puff at stroke midpoint on pen-up
- Open wall in two browser tabs (different accounts) → each sees the other's cursor dot + name
- Move mouse across canvas → cursor updates smoothly on other tab (~80ms intervals)
- Mouse leaves canvas → cursor disappears on other tab
- Close tab → cursor auto-cleans after 3s on other tab
- 10+ cursors render without lag (Konva Layer with `listening={false}`)
- Puff animations don't accumulate (cleaned up after 500ms)

# Plan: Freedom Wall Interaction Feedback & Live Cursors

## Context
The Freedom Wall canvas currently feels static — items appear instantly without visual feedback, and there's no sense of other users being present. Two improvements will make it feel alive: a "puff" particle effect when items are placed, and real-time cursor broadcasting so users can see each other moving around the wall.

## Files to Modify
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css`

---

## Feature 1: Placement Puff Animation

Visual particle burst when any item (sticker, stamp, note, doodle) is placed on the canvas.

### Implementation

**1. Add `placementPuffs` state array**
- Shape: `[{id, x, y, startedAt}]` — pixel coordinates, not normalized
- Each puff auto-removes after animation completes (~500ms)

**2. Create `PuffEffect` Konva component**
- Renders inside the items `<Layer>` (after all items, on top)
- Uses `useRef` + `requestAnimationFrame` loop to animate:
  - 5–8 small `<Circle>` particles that fly outward from center, shrinking + fading
  - 1 central expanding `<Ring>` that fades out
- Each particle gets a random angle, travels outward ~30-50px over 500ms
- Colors: soft purple/pink tones matching the accent (`#a21caf`, `#e879f9`)
- On animation complete, calls `onComplete(id)` to remove from state

**3. Trigger puffs on item creation**
- In `createItemMutation.onMutate`: calculate pixel position from the normalized `x, y` payload, push a new puff into state
- Convert normalized coords → pixel: `x * stageWidth`, `y * stageHeight`
- For doodles: use the midpoint of the drawn stroke

**4. Render puffs on canvas**
- After all items in the Layer, map `placementPuffs` → `<PuffEffect>` components
- Each `PuffEffect` self-animates and self-destructs via the `onComplete` callback

---

## Feature 2: Live Cursors via Supabase Broadcast

Show other users' cursor positions on the canvas in real-time.

### Implementation

**1. Generate a stable client ID**
- `useRef` initialized once: `fw-client-${Date.now()}-${random}`
- Used to filter out own cursor from rendering

**2. Extend the existing Supabase channel**
- The `freedom-wall-${weekId}` channel already exists for `postgres_changes`
- Chain `.on("broadcast", {event: "cursor-move"}, handler)` before `.subscribe()`
- No new channel needed

**3. Broadcast cursor position (throttled)**
- On `onMouseMove` / `onTouchMove` over the Stage (separate from doodle handling):
  - Compute normalized position `{x: px/stageWidth, y: py/stageHeight}`
  - Throttle to every **80ms** using a ref + setTimeout pattern (same as CanvasViewer's `queueDoodleBroadcast`)
  - Payload: `{clientId, userId, userName, x, y, tool: activeTool, timestamp}`
- On mouse leave Stage: broadcast a `cursor-leave` event to remove cursor
- Only broadcast when `canWrite` is true (logged-in users)

**4. Track remote cursors in state**
- `const [remoteCursors, setRemoteCursors] = useState({})` — keyed by clientId
- Shape: `{clientId, userId, userName, x, y, tool, updatedAt}`
- On `cursor-move` broadcast received: upsert into state (skip own clientId)
- On `cursor-leave` received: remove from state

**5. Auto-cleanup stale cursors**
- `setInterval` every 1.5s, remove cursors with `updatedAt` older than 3s
- Handles disconnects/tab-closes where `cursor-leave` never fires

**6. Render remote cursors on canvas**
- New `<Layer>` on top of the items layer (non-interactive: `listening={false}`)
- For each remote cursor:
  - Small colored `<Circle>` (radius 5, fill based on `userId` hash for consistent color)
  - `<Text>` label showing `userName` (truncated to 10 chars), offset slightly right+below the dot
  - Position: `x * stageWidth`, `y * stageHeight`
- Limit rendering to max 15 cursors (sorted by most recent `updatedAt`)

**7. Broadcast cleanup on unmount**
- Send `cursor-leave` event before channel teardown
- Clear interval timer

**8. CSS for cursor layer (HTML overlay for name labels — optional)**
- No CSS needed; all rendering is within Konva canvas layers

---

## Detailed Change Map

### State additions
```
const [placementPuffs, setPlacementPuffs] = useState([]);
const [remoteCursors, setRemoteCursors] = useState({});
const clientIdRef = useRef(`fw-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2,8)}`);
const cursorBroadcastTimerRef = useRef(null);
const lastCursorBroadcastRef = useRef(0);
const realtimeChannelRef = useRef(null);
```

### Channel modification (existing useEffect at line ~622)
- Add `.on("broadcast", {event: "cursor-move"}, handler)` and `.on("broadcast", {event: "cursor-leave"}, handler)` to the channel chain
- Store channel in `realtimeChannelRef` for broadcast sending
- On cleanup: send `cursor-leave`, then `removeChannel`

### New components (inside same file)
- `PuffEffect` — self-animating Konva Group with particles
- `RemoteCursor` — Konva Group with Circle + Text for one cursor

### New Konva Layer (after items Layer)
```jsx
<Layer listening={false}>
    {remoteCursorItems.map(cursor => <RemoteCursor key={cursor.clientId} ... />)}
</Layer>
```

### Puff trigger location
- Inside `createItemMutation.onMutate` callback, after creating the optimistic item:
```js
const pxX = (payload.payload.x || 0.5) * stageWidth;
const pxY = (payload.payload.y || 0.5) * stageHeight;
setPlacementPuffs(prev => [...prev, {id: optimisticId, x: pxX, y: pxY, startedAt: Date.now()}]);
```

### Cursor broadcast location
- New `handleCursorMove` called from Stage `onMouseMove` (runs alongside existing `handleStagePointerMove` for doodles — separate concern, both fire)
- Uses throttle pattern: skip if <80ms since last broadcast

---

## Verification
- Place a sticker/stamp/note → particle puff bursts at placement point, fades in ~500ms
- Draw a doodle → puff at stroke midpoint on pen-up
- Open wall in two browser tabs (different accounts) → each sees the other's cursor dot + name
- Move mouse across canvas → cursor updates smoothly on other tab (~80ms intervals)
- Mouse leaves canvas → cursor disappears on other tab
- Close tab → cursor auto-cleans after 3s on other tab
- 10+ cursors render without lag (Konva Layer with `listening={false}`)
- Puff animations don't accumulate (cleaned up after 500ms)

# Plan: Inline Note Creation on Freedom Wall Canvas

## Context
Currently, clicking the canvas with the "note" tool opens a modal overlay to design and type a note. The user wants notes to appear directly on the canvas instead, where they can be dragged to reposition while being designed via an inline panel. Character limit reduced from 800 to 200.

## Files to Modify
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css`

## Changes

### 1. Replace `noteModal` state with `draftNote` state
- Rename `noteModal` → `draftNote` (same shape: x, y, text, fontFamily, fontStyle, fontColor, bgColor, fontSize)
- Default `text` to `""` instead of `"New note"`

### 2. Render draft note directly on the Konva canvas
- Add a `NoteNode` in the items Layer (after all sorted items) when `draftNote` is set
- Pass `isDraft={true}` prop to `NoteNode` for visual distinction (dashed purple border)
- Make it draggable — `onDragEnd` updates `draftNote.x` and `draftNote.y`

### 3. Add `isDraft` prop to `NoteNode` component
- When `isDraft` is true: dashed stroke border on the main note body shape

### 4. Replace modal JSX with inline design panel
- Remove the full-screen modal overlay (`fw-note-modal-overlay`)
- Add an inline `fw-draft-note-panel` between toolbar and canvas with:
  - Character count display (X/200)
  - Textarea (maxLength=200, autoFocus)
  - Font, style, font color, note color, font size controls
  - "Place Note" (disabled when empty) and "Cancel" buttons
- All changes update `draftNote` state → canvas note updates live

### 5. Update handlers
- `handlePlaceItem`: set `draftNote` instead of `noteModal`, guard against double-drafting
- New `handleDraftNoteSave`: creates wall item from `draftNote`, clears it
- `handleStagePointerDown`: skip placing another note if `draftNote` already active

### 6. Auto-cancel draft on tool switch
- `useEffect` clears `draftNote` when `activeTool` changes away from `"note"`

### 7. Update character limits
- New draft textarea: `maxLength={200}`
- Existing note editor textarea: `maxLength={800}` → `maxLength={200}`

### 8. Add CSS for inline draft panel
- `.fw-draft-note-panel` — card-style, inline (not overlay), matches existing toolbar styling
- Responsive: controls stack vertically at 480px

### 9. Remove dead modal code
- Remove all `noteModal`/`setNoteModal` references
- Remove `handleNoteModalSave`
- Remove modal overlay JSX
- Remove modal CSS classes (`.fw-note-modal-*`)

## Verification
- Click canvas with note tool → note appears on canvas with dashed border, inline panel shows
- Type text → canvas note updates live
- Change font/color/style → canvas note reflects changes instantly
- Drag note on canvas → repositions correctly
- "Place Note" → saves to backend, draft clears, note persists
- "Cancel" → draft clears, no note saved
- Switch to another tool while drafting → draft auto-cancels
- Click canvas again while draft active → ignored (no duplicate drafts)
- Character count shows and enforces 200 char limit

  - Payload: `{clientId, userId, userName, x, y, tool: activeTool, timestamp}`
- On mouse leave Stage: broadcast a `cursor-leave` event to remove cursor
- Only broadcast when `canWrite` is true (logged-in users)

**4. Track remote cursors in state**
- `const [remoteCursors, setRemoteCursors] = useState({})` — keyed by clientId
- Shape: `{clientId, userId, userName, x, y, tool, updatedAt}`
- On `cursor-move` broadcast received: upsert into state (skip own clientId)
- On `cursor-leave` received: remove from state

**5. Auto-cleanup stale cursors**
- `setInterval` every 1.5s, remove cursors with `updatedAt` older than 3s
- Handles disconnects/tab-closes where `cursor-leave` never fires

**6. Render remote cursors on canvas**
- New `<Layer>` on top of the items layer (non-interactive: `listening={false}`)
- For each remote cursor:
  - Small colored `<Circle>` (radius 5, fill based on `userId` hash for consistent color)
  - `<Text>` label showing `userName` (truncated to 10 chars), offset slightly right+below the dot
  - Position: `x * stageWidth`, `y * stageHeight`
- Limit rendering to max 15 cursors (sorted by most recent `updatedAt`)

**7. Broadcast cleanup on unmount**
- Send `cursor-leave` event before channel teardown
- Clear interval timer

**8. CSS for cursor layer (HTML overlay for name labels — optional)**
- No CSS needed; all rendering is within Konva canvas layers
